title: Naples & Amalfi Coast 5-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Naples & Amalfi Coast 5-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Naples & Amalfi Coast, Italy: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Pompeii Archaeological Park and Herculaneum Archaeological Site. Prices from $1306 per person (estimate).
highlights: Sunset dinner overlooking Positano from a clifftop terrace
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Naples & Amalfi Coast, Italy — Mediterranean climate with hot summers (highs around 30°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 12-13°C in January); coastal towns feel cooler thanks to sea breezes. April to June and September to October for warm weather without peak-summer heat and traffic on the Amalfi Coast's narrow roads. This itinerary blends Pompeii Archaeological Park, Herculaneum Archaeological Site, Spaccanapoli with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Pompeii Archaeological Park, Herculaneum Archaeological Site, Spaccanapoli
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Naples & Amalfi Coast
itinerary.description: Land at Naples International Airport (NAP) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Pompeii Archaeological Park — Ancient Roman city preserved by Vesuvius' eruption. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Naples & Amalfi Coast: Herculaneum Archaeological Site & Mount Vesuvius crater hike
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Herculaneum Archaeological Site — Smaller, remarkably preserved Roman town. In the afternoon, continue to Mount Vesuvius crater hike — Guided hike to the rim of the still-active volcano. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Naples & Amalfi Coast: Naples National Archaeological Museum & Capodimonte Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Naples National Archaeological Museum — World-class collection of Pompeii and Herculaneum artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Capodimonte Museum — Royal palace and art collection set in a park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Capri island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Capri island, a popular side trip from Naples & Amalfi Coast. Return to Naples & Amalfi Coast in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Naples Underground (Napoli Sotterranea) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to NAP for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Pompeii tickets online and go early morning to beat heat and tour groups.
Take the ferry rather than the SITA bus between Amalfi Coast towns in peak season to skip road traffic.
Keep valuables secure and bags zipped in central Naples' crowded streets.
Book the Blue Grotto trip for a calm-sea morning — it closes in rough water.
Self-driving the Amalfi Coast road is stressful — most visitors do better with buses, ferries or a private driver.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable non-slip shoes for steep steps in Amalfi Coast towns and Pompeii's uneven streets
Swimwear and a cover-up for boat trips and beach clubs
A light rain jacket for spring/autumn showers
Sun protection for shade-free archaeological sites
Motion-sickness remedies for winding coastal bus rides
thingsToDo: Certosa e Museo di San Martino
Path of the Gods guided hike
Hiking the Path of the Gods
thingsToAvoid: Leaving bags or phones unattended in central Naples.
Driving a rental car on the Amalfi Coast's narrow hairpin roads in peak season without experience.
Visiting Pompeii or Vesuvius at midday in summer without water and a hat.
Assuming SITA buses run exactly on schedule — delays in traffic are common.
Skipping Herculaneum in favor of only Pompeii — it is smaller but better preserved and far less crowded.

festivalCalendar.name: Feast of San Gennaro
festivalCalendar.timing: September 19, the miracle of the liquefying blood of Naples' patron saint

festivalCalendar.name: Ravello Festival
festivalCalendar.timing: July-August, music festival on the Amalfi Coast

festivalCalendar.name: Regatta of the Ancient Maritime Republics
festivalCalendar.timing: Rotating date, historic ceremonial boat race

festivalCalendar.name: San Gregorio Armeno Christmas Nativity tradition
festivalCalendar.timing: December, in Naples' artisan nativity-scene street

localEtiquette: Neapolitans are expressive and loud in a friendly way — don't mistake volume for hostility.
Dress modestly when entering churches along the coast.
Pizza in Naples is traditionally eaten with a fork and knife; folding it is more of a street-food style.
Keep bags zipped and valuables close in crowded central Naples streets.
Greet shopkeepers warmly; light haggling is acceptable in street markets, not in fixed-price shops.
localTransportGuide: The Circumvesuviana train connects Naples to Pompeii, Herculaneum and Sorrento cheaply, while the SITA bus is the classic way to travel the narrow, cliffside Amalfi Coast road between towns. Ferries from Naples, Sorrento or Positano are often faster and more comfortable than road travel in peak season, especially to Capri.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October for warm weather without peak-summer heat and traffic on the Amalfi Coast's narrow roads.
weather: Mediterranean climate with hot summers (highs around 30°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 12-13°C in January); coastal towns feel cooler thanks to sea breezes.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Naples & Amalfi Coast?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Naples & Amalfi Coast?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October for warm weather without peak-summer heat and traffic on the Amalfi Coast's narrow roads. Mediterranean climate with hot summers (highs around 30°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 12-13°C in January); coastal towns feel cooler thanks to sea breezes.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Naples & Amalfi Coast safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Naples & Amalfi Coast is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Naples & Amalfi Coast?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Naples & Amalfi Coast is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset dinner overlooking Positano from a clifftop terrace experience.

reviews.author: James Cohen
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Naples & Amalfi Coast was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Mount Vesuvius crater hike was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Wei Sharma
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Amalfi's Duomo di Sant'Andrea steps in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sofia Brown
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Amalfi's Duomo di Sant'Andrea steps in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Ibrahim
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Piazza del Plebiscito and Positano viewpoint. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Nadia Novak
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Naples & Amalfi Coast is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Path of the Gods guided hike. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Tom Kim
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Certosa e Museo di San Martino and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Marco Ibrahim
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Herculaneum Archaeological Site and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mohammed Wang
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Naples Underground (Napoli Sotterranea) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Herculaneum Archaeological Site this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
